author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
How Our Nature Could Be Vitiated by Sin, Even Though It Be Not a Substance.
title: How Our Nature Could Be Vitiated by Sin, Even Though It Be Not a Substance.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Chapter 22 [XX.]—How Our Nature Could Be Vitiated by Sin, Even Though It Be Not a Substance.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Now, do you not perceive the tendency and direction of this controversy?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Even to render of none effect the Scripture where it is said “Thou shalt call His name Jesus, for He shall save His people from their sins.”

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: For how is He to save where there is no malady?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: For the sins, from which this gospel says Christ’s people have to be saved, are not substances, and according to this writer are incapable of corrupting.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: O brother, how good a thing it is to remember that you are a Christian!

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: To believe, might perhaps be enough; but still, since you persist in discussion, there is no harm, nay there is even benefit, if a firm faith precede it; let us not suppose, then, that human nature cannot be corrupted by sin, but rather, believing, from the inspired Scriptures, that it is corrupted by sin, let our inquiry be how this could possibly have come about.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Since, then, we have already learnt that sin is not a substance, do we not consider, not to mention any other example, that not to eat is also not a substance?

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: Because such abstinence is withdrawal from a substance, inasmuch as food is a substance.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: To abstain, then, from food is not a substance; and yet the substance of our body, if it does altogether abstain from food, so languishes, is so impaired by broken health, is so exhausted of strength, so weakened and broken with very weariness, that even if it be in any way able to continue alive, it is hardly capable of being restored to the use of that food, by abstaining from which it became so corrupted and injured.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: In the same way sin is not a substance; but God is a substance, yea the height of substance and only true sustenance of the reasonable creature.

verses.book: A Treatise on Nature and Grace.
verses.text: The consequence of departing from Him by disobedience, and of inability, through infirmity, to receive what one ought really to rejoice in, you hear from the Psalmist, when he says: “My heart is smitten and withered like grass, since I have forgotten to eat my bread.”
